GURRAGTER creatIoN
LIFE PATH The Life Path is where your character takes shape_ By mak- ing choices and following the instructions for each choice, you lead your character   through childhood schooling  ad early career. Going through the Life Path not only gives you finished character but also puts you well on your way toward completing your character'= personal history: The Life Path is divided into four stages that represent dif- ferent parts of your character" life: Early Childhood Stage Late Childhood (Stage 21, Higher Education (Stage 3) and Real Life (Stage 4). Each stage contains several different paths that represent specific kinds of upbringing; education and so on_ For example , in the Higher Education Stage_ you can choose the University Path or opt for Military Academy: Specific Life Paths begin on p. 33_ In general, you simply choose path for your character at each stage and follow the instructions for that path; exceptions are noted in the text: The various paths provide your growing character with skills traits and other characteristics based on the kind of life he or she leading at given stage_
Any subpath described as part of this pass under Next Path may be taken immediately after that path or subpath_ with: out moving on to the next stage Alternatively; character returns to path at later point; he or she may take the sub path on this subsequent pass (see Repeating Paths, p. 251.
Path Limits Player characters should experience the highlights of their careers during game play: not while going through the Life Path Therefore , your character can follow limited number of paths before beginning play: Every character goes through one Early Childhood and one Late Childhood path_ After that; character can take up t0 six additional paths_ from any combination of the Higher Education and Real Life stages (as appropriate to his Or her affiliation ad the prerequisites of each path). Mandatory subpaths such as military Advanced Individual Training (AIT) and University Majors are not considered separate paths purposes ofthis rule_ However; elective   subpaths such as military Special Training and University Schools are con- sidered separate. path'$ status is unclear; the gamemaster decides whether specific subpath counts mandatory elective based on the path and character in question; character can exceed the given limit with the gamemas ter'$ permission, but the gamemaster should keep in mind that longer career will result in more skilled (though older) char acter. Before requesting this option; players should be advised that the Life Path can become increasingly dangerous for those who loiter (see Repeating Paths_ p: 251. Rather than starting play as seasoned veteran; players might prefer to skip those extra tours Of duty for the sake of their characters well-being: There' plenty of time to become seasoned during game playl
FOLLOWING THE PATHS Each time your character follows path, he or she may receive skills and traits (p. 23), or the path may affect the char acter's Attributes (below). Each path also takes Up certain amount time, which affects character $ age (p. 591. Additionally; events (p_ can occur that may alter the course of your character' life Once you have resolved the effects of one path on your character, you have several options for the path YOu may follow next (you may only take one of these options). These options appear under the Next Path header in each path' description: The life stage in which the character follows the path appears after each path name in parentheses The Next Path listing does not take into account path restrictions that your character must follow: Path restrictions may result from certain events your character' s affiliations (see p 26). This especially true of Clan characters, who fol: low special set of Life Path rules; Clan path restrictions appear under the Clan Characters heading in each stage_ Some paths have entry requirements called prerequisites: In most cases the prerequisite is certain affiliation, though some paths require the character t0 have obtained certain trait or not t0 have certain trait in order to enter. Prerequisites for each path are shown in italics directly below the path title _
ATTRIBUTES AND THE LIFE PATH character's life can have serious impact on his physi- cal, mental and emotional development, The Life Path reflects this through the modification of character' $ Attribute   mini- mums and thresholds: These affect the purchasing and assign: ing of Attributes set of numbers that represent character' basic physical and mental abilities_ general fortunes and social standing: (For more detailed information on Attributes ad how to use them; see 54, Players can keep track of their Attribute minimums and thresholds on the Character Creation Worksheet in the back of the book (p 218).
Attribute Minimums Some paths require character to have a Attribute at certain level in order t0 enter: for example the Nobility Path (in the Early Childhood stage). Because character must be of noble birth in order to take this path_ the path requires mini: mum Social Standing Attribute of 5 When assigning specific Attributes later in character creation_ player whose character has taken this path must assign minimum value of 5 to his character's Social Standing Attribute t0 reflect the character'$ noble birth.
Subpaths Some paths include' various subpaths that represent extension or specialized focus of the path in question. In many ways_ subpaths function as variants on the main path. Some subpaths are mandatory; characters must take them after fol: lowing the main path (as indicated under Next Path) . Other sub paths are optional and may be taken if the player desires Like paths, subpaths often have prerequisites
